blocks.content: Can I tell you something I haven't said out loud yet?
I think I've been in love with you since the fountain. I didn't let myself say it because it felt like something I wasn't allowed to have. Not with this. Not with all the complications of this.
But tonight on that wheel I thought — what if the complication is exactly the reason to say it? What if the reason isn't despite everything, but because of it?
I love you, Cass. I have for a while. I've been waiting for the right moment to say it and then tonight I realized there's no such thing as the right moment. There's just the moment you're in.
That's very wise for someone sitting in a parking lot.
I've been out here a while.
In a minute. I want to stay here a little longer.

They texted back and forth for another hour about nothing in particular — a song she thought he should hear, something Nathan had said that she'd been thinking about, the exact temperature of a good carnival night. The ordinary things. The things that hold you.
Her last message came at 1:17 in the morning.
Goodnight. I'm glad you were at the Copper Mug that day.
